concerns.text: T029 (Sensitivity Analysis) is placed in Phase 5 (US3) but depends on the output of T020 (Data Cleaning) and T021 (ANOVA/Contrasts) from Phase 4 (US2). The current ordering implies US3 can start after Foundational, but T029 explicitly requires the primary analysis results to sweep thresholds. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule; US3 tasks must be ordered after US2 completion, not just after Foundational.

T008 and T009 (Tests for US1) are listed in Phase 3 alongside implementation tasks. While the text says 'Write these tests FIRST', the linear task list places them after T004-T007 (Foundational) but before T010-T017. However, T008 depends on T005 (Randomization Logic) being implemented. T005 is in Phase 2 (Foundational), so T008 is correctly placed after T005. However, T009 (Integration test for session flow) depends on T010-T012 (Interface implementations) to exist to test the flow. Listing T009 before T010-T012 in the same phase block creates a semantic ordering violation where the consumer (test) precedes the producer (implementation).

T032 (Post-hoc power calculation) is listed in Phase 5. This task logically depends on the results of the primary analysis (T021/T022) and the final dataset size determined after T020 (Data Cleaning). Placing it in the same phase block as T028 (Pre-study power analysis) without explicit dependency on US2 completion risks a consumer-before-producer violation if the pre-study and post-hoc tasks are executed in parallel or out of order.
